observations: Wordcount hunt: cut the opener's orphaned child-reaction tail so the road damage lands faster.
Wordcount hunt: trimmed Sylvanas's road-watch list to keep the POV tactical rather than panoramic.
Wordcount hunt: removed 'whole' from Hearthglen's reveal; the line did not need the extra qualifier.
Wordcount hunt: stripped the pause before Tomas's name so the refugee exchange moves at field speed.
Wordcount hunt: reduced the triage inventory by one prop and one setup clause.
Wordcount hunt: compressed the aftermath after Sylvanas drops the first corpse; one cry was enough.
Wordcount hunt: removed the bridge sentence announcing Uther before the scene already showed him.
Wordcount hunt: cut the unloaded-wagon inventory beat because Uther's next line already cashes out the logistics pressure.
Wordcount hunt: tightened the command-pavilion setup by dropping one map-detail sentence.
Wordcount hunt: cut one reaction sentence before Arthas speaks; the room already had enough pressure.
Wordcount hunt: shortened the camp-smell paragraph by trimming redundant phrasing.
Wordcount hunt: cut the final road panorama down to the load-bearing geography only.
Hitlist hunt: replaced 'His voice had gravel in it' with 'His voice scraped' to avoid prefab phrasing.
Hitlist hunt: replaced 'worn to the limit' with 'used up' for plainer in-scene diction.
Hitlist hunt: replaced 'quick and built' with 'quick and precise' so Jaina sounds like herself instead of a line editor.
Hitlist hunt: replaced 'grip altered' with 'grip tightened' to kill abstract body-language narration.
Hitlist hunt: replaced 'took a second look' with 'looked back' to cut report-like phrasing.
Hitlist hunt: replaced the knife simile with 'blade-out' so the title tension stays sharp without comparison-fog.
Hitlist hunt: replaced 'trying to ruin it' with 'trying to undo it' to keep Sylvanas dry instead of melodramatic.
Hitlist hunt: replaced 'disciplined eyes' with 'steady eyes' to kill elegant-military overreach on a horse beat.
Hitlist hunt: kept the two literal breath references in Uther's healing beat; both are physical respiration, not emotional breath-tic padding.
Hitlist hunt: left the three filter-word hits untouched because all three are load-bearing dialogue/report verbs, not limp narration-distance tics.
Dialogue hunt: checked every quoted line against document-voice and academic-register contamination; no spoken line needed a rewrite.
Dialogue hunt: Jaina's technical speech stayed within her voice sheet's plain-language ceiling for non-mages; no lab-report jargon survived.
Dialogue hunt: Sylvanas, Uther, and Arthas all sounded rank-specific and in-scene; the register sweep was clean after verification.

changes.before: Children sat on sacks and watched riders go the wrong way.
the road, the wagons, the people, and Jaina's profile
changes.after: the road, the wagons, and Jaina's profile

changes.before: a horse trough dragged over as a wash basin
By the time the body stopped moving, another cry had gone up beyond the tents. Another stretcher.
changes.after: Another cry went up beyond the tents.

changes.before: That was where they found Uther.
His voice had gravel in it.
He looked worn to the limit.
Her voice came out quick and built.
changes.after: Her voice came out quick and precise.

changes.before: Even Uther's grip altered on the hammer haft.
changes.after: Even Uther's grip tightened on the hammer haft.

changes.before: Falric checked Jaina, then Arthas, and took a second look.
changes.after: Falric checked Jaina, then Arthas, and looked back.

changes.before: The title he'd chosen for her was still in the room between them like a knife left out on purpose.
changes.after: The title he'd chosen for her still lay between them, blade-out.

changes.before: Jaina was already trying to ruin it.
changes.after: Jaina was already trying to undo it.

changes.before: Mud, trampled straw, and smoke had worked themselves into one camp smell.
changes.after: Mud, trampled straw, and smoke had become one camp smell.

changes.before: the Stratholme road ran between drainage ditches, winter-cut fields, and low barns that fed market carts north when the world still obeyed the season.
changes.after: the Stratholme road ran between drainage ditches, winter-cut fields, and low barns.

flags: Literal breath references in Uther's healing beat were kept; they describe actual respiration, not generic emotion markers.
Uther's 'I had noticed' stayed; dry understatement fits character voice and does not read as document register.
Three raw 'something' matches remain, all in ordinary speech/action syntax rather than vague-intensifier narration.
Dialogue register sweep found no clinical, bureaucratic, or academic contamination severe enough to justify a spoken-line rewrite.
